body:
### 🐛 Bug Description

Context: Zed editor using the openai_compatible provider pointed at Shimmy. When attaching a file via @filename in the Agent Panel, Zed sends the OpenAI multi-part content array format (per the [OpenAI Chat Completions spec](https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/chat/create)). Shimmy rejects this with a 422.

Error message received:

```
HTTP response error from Shimmy's API: status 422 Unprocessable Entity - 
"Failed to deserialize the JSON body into the target type: 
messages[1].content: invalid type: sequence, expected a string at line 1 column 7254"
```

Root cause: Shimmy's message deserializer only accepts content as a String, but the OpenAI spec allows content to be either a String or an array of content parts (text, image_url, etc.). This is the standard format used by Zed, Cursor, Continue, and other clients when attaching file context.

Request: Support the OpenAI-spec union type for content:
content: string | array<{ type: "text"|"image_url", text: string, ... }>





### 🔄 Steps to Reproduce

```bash
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:11435/v1/chat/completions   -H 'Content-Type: application/json'   -d '{
    "model": "qwen-qwen3-14b-q4-k-m",
    "messages": [
      {
        "role": "system",
        "content": "You are a helpful assistant."
      },
      {
        "role": "user",
        "content": [
          {
            "type": "text",
            "text": "How do I specify the terraform version in this file?"
          },
          {
            "type": "text",
            "text": "```hcl\n# Contents of main.tf\nterraform {\n  required_providers {\n    aws = {\n      source = \"hashicorp/aws\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```"
          }
        ]
      }
    ],
    "max_tokens": 512,
    "stream": false
  }'
```

Error: Failed to deserialize the JSON body into the target type: messages[1].content: invalid type: sequence, expected a string at line 10 column 19

Alternatively if using Zed, just try to attach a file using the "@file" along with the command.

### ✅ Expected Behavior

When attaching content using "@file", it should be able to utilize that content for execution of the command. 

### ❌ Actual Behavior

In Zed, HTTP response error from Shimmy's API: status 422 Unprocessable Entity - 
"Failed to deserialize the JSON body into the target type: 
messages[1].content: invalid type: sequence, expected a string at line 1 column 7254"

From the curl command:
Failed to deserialize the JSON body into the target type: messages[1].content: invalid type: sequence, expected a string at line 10 column 19

### 📦 Shimmy Version

Latest (main branch)

### 💻 Operating System

macOS

### 📥 Installation Method

Pre-built binary from releases

### 🌍 Environment Details

- Model: qwen-qwen3-14b-q4-k-m

### 📋 Logs/Error Messages

```text
From Zed, status 422 Unprocessable Entity - 
"Failed to deserialize the JSON body into the target type: 
messages[1].content: invalid type: sequence, expected a string at line 1 column 7254"

From the curl command:
Failed to deserialize the JSON body into the target type: messages[1].content: invalid type: sequence, expected a string at line 10 column 19
```

### 📝 Additional Context

_No response_

body:
### 🐛 Bug Description

Environment variables have no impact on the application’s configuration. CLI flags work correctly.

### 🔄 Steps to Reproduce

1. Create `compose.yaml` file

```yaml
services:
  shimmy:
    build: https://github.com/Michael-A-Kuykendall/shimmy.git#v1.8.2
    ports:
      - "11434:11434"  # Shimmy server port
    environment:
      - SHIMMY_BASE_GGUF=/app/models      # Point to mounted models
      - SHIMMY_PORT=11434                 # Server port
      - SHIMMY_HOST=0.0.0.0               # Listen on all interfaces
```

2. `docker compose up shimmy`
3. Try `curl localhost:11434/health`

### ✅ Expected Behavior

`🚀 Starting server on 0.0.0.0:11434`

### ❌ Actual Behavior

`🚀 Starting server on 127.0.0.1:11435`

### 📦 Shimmy Version

v1.4.0 or newer

### 💻 Operating System

Docker

### 📥 Installation Method

Docker/Docker Compose

### 🌍 Environment Details

Inside the Docker shell
```
root@bdac42acd2f9:/app# export
declare -x HOME="/root"
declare -x HOSTNAME="bdac42acd2f9"
declare -x OLDPWD
declare -x PATH="/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin"
declare -x PWD="/app"
declare -x SHIMMY_BASE_GGUF="/app/models"
declare -x SHIMMY_HOST="0.0.0.0"
declare -x SHIMMY_PORT="11434"
declare -x SHLVL="1"
declare -x TERM="xterm"
```

### 📋 Logs/Error Messages

```text
shimmy-1  | 🎯 Shimmy v1.8.2
shimmy-1  | 🔧 Backend: CPU (no GPU acceleration)
shimmy-1  | 📦 Models: 0 available
shimmy-1  | 🚀 Starting server on 127.0.0.1:11435
shimmy-1  | 📦 Models: 24 available
shimmy-1  | ✅ Ready to serve requests
shimmy-1  |    • POST /api/generate (streaming + non-streaming)
shimmy-1  |    • GET  /health (health check + metrics)
shimmy-1  |    • GET  /v1/models (OpenAI-compatible)
```

### 📝 Additional Context

- The lines with `📦 Models` are duplicated in format — both use the same `📦 Models` label (one shows `0` available, the other `24` available).
- As a workaround, instead of using the Docker environment, you can use `command: ["shimmy", "serve", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:11434"]`
- There is no Dockerfile to build and verify the GPU acceleration

body:
## Problem Description

Users cannot install shimmy with MLX support through standard distribution channels despite fixes existing in source code.

## User Reports from Issue #68

**Users Affected**: @zsyo, @newnight, @D0wn10ad

**Current Distribution Status**:
❌ **crates.io**: Still on v1.5.1 (missing MLX template fixes)  
❌ **Homebrew**: Still on v1.5.5 (no MLX support)  
❌ **cargo install shimmy --features mlx**: Still fails with template errors  

## Expected Behavior

Users should be able to install shimmy with MLX support via:
- `cargo install shimmy --features mlx`
- `brew install shimmy` (with MLX on Apple Silicon)

## Actual Behavior

**crates.io Package**:
- Latest version: v1.5.1 
- Missing template fixes
- `cargo install` fails with template compilation errors

**Homebrew Formula**:
- Latest version: v1.5.5
- Missing MLX support compilation
- `shimmy gpu-info` shows "MLX Backend: Disabled"

## Required Actions

1. **Publish Real v1.5.6+ to crates.io** with template fixes and MLX support
2. **Update Homebrew formula** to use MLX-enabled build
3. **Verify installation** works through both channels
4. **Test MLX detection** on Apple Silicon after installation

## Root Cause

Distribution pipeline is not properly releasing source code fixes to package managers. Users experience:
- Source code has fixes
- Released packages don't include fixes
- "Resolved" issues that users still can't use

## Impact

Users cannot access MLX functionality through standard installation methods, leading to justified criticism about incomplete resolutions.

## Labels

- bug
- distribution
- mlx
- packaging

## Problem Description

Users report that popular LLM frontends cannot integrate with shimmy despite working with Ollama.

## User Report from Issue #66

**User**: @barseghyanartur  
**Status**: CURL works but frontends fail  

**Working**:
✅ Direct CURL requests to shimmy API  
✅ Both frontends work seamlessly with Ollama  

**Not Working**:
❌ Open WebUI cannot detect/use shimmy  
❌ AnythingLLM cannot detect/use shimmy  

## Expected Behavior

Popular LLM frontends should work with shimmy's OpenAI-compatible API just like they do with Ollama.

## Actual Behavior

- **Open WebUI**: Cannot detect shimmy as model provider
- **AnythingLLM**: Refuses to automatically detect shimmy model provider
- Both tools work immediately with Ollama on same system

## Technical Investigation Required

1. **API Response Format**: Are shimmy's responses matching OpenAI specification exactly?
2. **Models Endpoint**: Does `/v1/models` return the format these frontends expect?
3. **Chat Completions**: Are response fields complete and properly formatted?
4. **CORS/Authentication**: Are there connection issues preventing frontend access?
5. **Model Detection**: How do these frontends discover available models?

## Compatibility Gap Analysis

Since both frontends work with Ollama but not shimmy, this suggests shimmy's OpenAI compatibility implementation has gaps that prevent real-world frontend integration.

## Impact

This blocks users from using shimmy with popular LLM interfaces, limiting adoption compared to Ollama.

## Affected Users

- @barseghyanartur (Issue #66)

## Related Issues

- Issue #66: Original Open WebUI integration question
- Issue #70: Feature implementation tracking

Hey @barseghyanartur and @DecayingSec!

Thanks for the detailed reports and for sticking with this - I really appreciate the patience while we got to the bottom of this compatibility issue.

The problem was actually quite subtle but critical: shimmy's /v1/models endpoint was returning extra fields that aren't part of the official OpenAI API specification. Frontend applications like Open WebUI and AnythingLLM do strict validation of the API responses, and those extra fields were causing them to reject shimmy as incompatible.

Specifically, shimmy was including root, parent, and permission fields in the model objects, but the OpenAI spec only allows id, object, created, and owned_by.

I've fixed this by:
- Creating a separate ListModel struct that matches the OpenAI spec exactly
- Updating the /v1/models endpoint to only return the standard fields
- Fixing the timestamps to use proper system time instead of 0
- Ensuring all tests validate the correct response format

The API now returns responses that are 100% compatible with OpenAI's specification, which should resolve the integration issues with Open WebUI, AnythingLLM, SillyTavern, and Zed.

This fix has been tested thoroughly - all release gates pass and the OpenAI compatibility tests are green. Give it a try with the latest main branch or wait for the next release!

Thanks again for the reports - these kinds of compatibility issues are crucial for adoption, and your feedback really helps make shimmy better for everyone.

Mike
